sanskrit:
शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च ।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
transliteration:
śamo damas tapaḥ śaucaṃ kṣāntir ārjavam eva ca |
jñānaṃ vijñānam āstikyaṃ brahma-karma svabhāva-jam ||
literal_meaning: Peacefulness, self-control, austerity, purity, tolerance, honesty, wisdom, knowledge, and faith are the natural-born duties of a brahmaṇa.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the nine intrinsic qualities that define the psychological and spiritual temperament of the contemplative (brahmaṇa) archetype—qualities that stabilize the mind for self-inquiry and service.
psychological_state: A calm, disciplined, and reflective mindset that values truth and inner purity over external achievement.
trigger_scenario: A software engineer chooses a lower-paying research role to preserve intellectual integrity and daily meditation practice.
anchor_text: Cultivate inner calm, self-mastery, and truth to steady the mind for wisdom and service.
